name: Paul Desmond
abstract: Paul Desmond (born Paul Emil Breitenfeld; November 25, 1924 – May 30, 1977) was an American jazz alto saxophonist and composer and proponent of cool jazz. He was a member of the Dave Brubeck Quartet and composed the group's biggest hit, "Take Five".

about_quotes.speaker: Bill Dobbins
about_quotes.evidence: I was fortunate to get it just a couple of years ago that I’ve been playing for my students at least once in every class... there’s one of them, my favorite, is an interview that Paul Desmond did with Charlie Parker for this radio show.
about_quotes.doc_title: Bill Dobbins interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rochester, New York, January 11, 2012

about_quotes.speaker: Ernie Watts
about_quotes.evidence: I heard Paul Desmond. And that was very encouraging for me because I was just starting to play... Paul Desmond played so lyrically and so song-like there were things that he played that I could play, that I could copy.
about_quotes.doc_title: Ernie Watts interviewed by Monk Rowe, Toronto, Canada, January 11, 2003

about_quotes.speaker: Mike Longo
about_quotes.evidence: Charlie Parker — there’s a famous interview between Paul Desmond and Charlie Parker where he says...
about_quotes.doc_title: Mike Longo interviewed by Monk Rowe, New York City, New York, January 13, 2019

about_quotes.speaker: Greg Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: Because I loved Phil Woods and his playing. Because I loved Paul Desmond. I loved Zoot Sims.
about_quotes.doc_title: Greg Thomas interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, October 11, 2014

about_quotes.speaker: Hentoff Nat
about_quotes.evidence: The second wife was introduced to me by Paul Desmond, who was a good friend of mine.
about_quotes.doc_title: Hentoff Nat — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Iola Brubeck
about_quotes.evidence: the addition of Paul [Desmond] to what had basically been a trio came about because of that injury... So to add Paul... then the burden was divided so that Paul could carry some of the load.
about_quotes.doc_title: Iola Brubeck interviewed by Monk Rowe, Wilton, CT, July 17, 2011
